meta.name: grcforge PCI-extended crosswalk (demo)
meta.note: Original demo crosswalk authored by Cognis Digital. Control identifiers (NIST AC-2, CIS 5.1, SOC 2 CC6.1, PCI DSS v4.0 requirement numbers such as 8.2 and 8.4) are factual references to public frameworks. All descriptive text is original; no framework prose is reproduced. PCI requirement numbers follow PCI DSS v4.0 numbering.

frameworks.cis: CIS Critical Security Controls v8 safeguards
frameworks.soc2: AICPA SOC 2 Trust Services Criteria (Common Criteria)

mappings.description: Assign every person a distinct identity so actions can be attributed to an individual.
Require more than one authentication factor for access into the cardholder data environment.
Limit access to system components and data to only those who need it for a business reason.
Render stored sensitive account data unreadable using approved cryptography.
Protect account data with strong cryptography when transmitted over open or public networks.
Record access to system components and account data so events can be reconstructed.
Review logs regularly and retain them for a defined period, protected from change.
Identify and rank security weaknesses and remediate them on a risk-based cadence.
Deploy and maintain mechanisms that detect and respond to malicious software.
Install and maintain controls between trusted and untrusted networks to isolate the cardholder data environment.
